industries.name: Nuclear Medicine Imaging
industries.share: ~95% of technetium use — essentially the only commercial application
uses: Technetium-99m (⁹⁹ᵐTc, metastable isomer, t½ = 6.01 hours) is the world's most widely used medical radioisotope — involved in approximately 40 million diagnostic nuclear medicine procedures annually worldwide

⁹⁹ᵐTc gamma emission at 140 keV is nearly ideal for gamma camera (SPECT) imaging: energetic enough to escape tissue, low enough to be efficiently detected by NaI(Tl) scintillation detectors, and short enough half-life to minimize patient radiation dose
Bone scans (⁹⁹ᵐTc-MDP): detects bone metastases, fractures, and osteomyelitis — the most common nuclear medicine procedure globally, used in cancer staging and orthopedic diagnosis
Myocardial perfusion imaging (⁹⁹ᵐTc-sestamibi, Cardiolite): assesses coronary artery disease and cardiac blood flow; used alongside stress testing to identify regions of ischemia in the heart

⁹⁹ᵐTc is produced from molybdenum-99 (⁹⁹Mo, t½ = 66 hours) generators — hospitals elute ⁹⁹ᵐTc from the generator column daily as sodium pertechnetate, then kit-label with various targeting ligands for specific organ imaging

companies.name: SHINE Medical Technologies
companies.use: US-based Mo-99 producer using accelerator-based neutron irradiation of uranium solution — a non-reactor method providing domestic US supply of Mo-99 for Tc-99m generators. Reduces US dependence on aging foreign research reactors.

companies.name: NorthStar Medical Radioisotopes
companies.use: Produces Mo-99 using neutron capture on stable Mo-98 targets in research reactors — carrier-added Mo-99 with lower specific activity. Supplies US domestic Tc-99m generator market as an alternative to foreign reactor supply.

companies.name: IRE (Institut National des Radioéléments)
companies.use: Major European Mo-99 producer using fission of highly enriched uranium (HEU) targets irradiated in the BR2 research reactor at SCK-CEN, Mol, Belgium. Supplies Mo-99 to Tc-99m generator manufacturers across Europe and globally.

companies.name: Curium (formerly IBA Molecular / Mallinckrodt)
companies.use: Major nuclear medicine radiopharmaceutical company. Manufactures Tc-99m generators (TechneLite, Drytec) distributed to hospitals for daily elution of Tc-99m for bone, cardiac, and other diagnostic imaging procedures.
